summary: PRD V2 battery round 3 — unaudited surfaces (a11y, perf/memory, renderer/reveal-engine, QA-bot rigor, economy/estate/quests) + devil-advocate REFUTE-pass on blocking B1-B7 + completeness-critic; reports NEW actionable + dryness
logs: round3: 49 findings, 35 NEW actionable, 9 refutations, verifying 11 new crit/high

new_blocking: B8 (CRITICAL, new — same accrual-machinery family as B1/B4/B5): NO costed/itemized/time-budgeted/schema-representable supply for the great/excellent gate bands, so EVERY v1 tier-gate is unclearable-as-costed. The ONLY supply the PRD costs tops out at GOOD: §4.2.1 deeds = exactly 70% of good (itemized line-by-line, verified verbatim) and §4.2.2 seasonal hard-capped at 30% of good (telescopes to JUDGE_K·1 = 0.30·good, can NEVER exceed good). But §4.1 gates need supra-good (T0 good+excellent-in-1; T1 great-in-2; T2 Estate/Office great/excellent — e.g. Estate excellent 112K vs costed 60K = +52K, Office great 78K vs 50K = +28K). The surplus is uncosted on every axis: ip-uncounted (the §4.1 'great/excellent specialisation deed-counts' lever is unset; Q23 'more deeds welcome, no fixed budget'), TIME-unbudgeted (§4.8.1b Phase-2 window is 100% consumed buying GOOD), schema-unrepresentable (§2.12 Quest has no repeatable/instance-count/maxAwards field), and verifier-untieable (§6.6.1 asserts BOTH the 70%-of-good accrual tie-out AND supra-good gate-reachability — mutually exclusive under one itemized inventory). FIX needs human design+balance+schema: (a) quantify the great/excellent surplus deed-counts per pillar/tier; (b) add the Phase-2-window minutes they cost; (c) add a Quest/AccrualEvent repeatable+maxAwards field + deed-completion ledger so 'more capped deeds' is bounded and representable; (d) redefine §6.6.1 so reachability of the ACTUAL required band (great/excellent) is a Phase-2-window TIME budget (auto-repeat-rate × window ≥ surplus), and reword accrual tie-out so 'gate share' doesn't read as 'good share'. docs/prd.md §4.1 L3336-3360, §4.2.1 L3484-3536, §4.2.2 L3591-3623, §4.8.1b, §2.12 L1769, §6.6.1 L5944-5949.

new_trivial_fixes.where: prd.md §6.4 L5789 / §2.1 L1182 / core/log; persisted §6.8 L6052
new_trivial_fixes.fix: Event log is the ONLY playtime-scaling save field yet its cap is described 'capped'/'ring buffer'/'append, cap' in 6+ places with NO number, and is written to 3 backends per autosave against 'keeps the save tiny' (§6.4 L5823). Pin a hard LOG_RING_MAX (~300) enforced as true ring eviction in core/log, persist only a small tail (~50 lines) decoupled from the display ring, and add a §6.6 save-envelope-size budget assert (≤~64KB typical). NOTE: a ring is constant-size once full — the round-3 '60h runaway/quota-fail' framing is refuted; this is a static save-size-budget gap, not a time-dependent leak.

new_trivial_fixes.where: prd.md §6.3 per-tick contract L5719-5723 / §6.4 L5787 effects[]
new_trivial_fixes.fix: `effects: ActiveEffect[]` is persisted (§6.8 L6053) but the per-tick contract is SILENT on expiry — the only persisted collection that is neither content-cardinality-bounded nor explicitly capped (unlike the carefully-capped log). Add one clause: tick decrements remainingTicks and DROPS expired effects; add an effects.length-bounded invariant. (Severity-overstated in round 3: the field is named 'with remaining duration' and an EffectId-keyed refresh-stacking model would self-bound; this is a spec-completeness hole, not a guaranteed leak.)

Reveal truth is doubly-specified — stored monotonic `unlocked` Set ('the ONLY reveal state') vs §6.9 renderer 'shows what the predicates CURRENTLY pass'. §2.1 L1175 permits non-monotonic predicate inputs (season/resources/rank/pillar) that all FALL in play, so a literal level-triggered read would UN-reveal panels (violating 'no reset, ever'). Make the stored Set the single authority: reduce/tick only ADD ids (write-once, never remove); reword §6.4-selectors+§6.9 to 'reads the latched Set'; unify the §6.2 isUnlocked/visibleSurfaces name; add a §6.6.1 guard that a surface never leaves `unlocked`. Intent is unambiguous (monotonic latch) — mechanical fix, but fix before M0 since `unlocked` is persisted.

FU4 proves anti-dump at TICK granularity ('no two collide on a single tick') but render(state,prevState) reconciles per FRAME and tick(dtTicks) advances K>1 ticks; the PRD never says what the renderer does when ONE reconciliation surfaces K>1 new surfaces. Clarify: FU4 bans only a PERSISTED GameState revealQueue, not renderer-local transient fade sequencing — specify the renderer sequences fade-ins when one reconciliation surfaces K>1 surfaces, and QA fast-forward jumps render the destination directly (exempt). (Round-3 over-claimed live-play vectors: backgrounding pauses, L6173.)

new_trivial_fixes.where: prd.md §4.2.2 Estate basis L3581 vs §4.2.3 working form L3673-3684
new_trivial_fixes.fix: §4.2.2 defines the Estate seasonal basis with the CIRCULAR min(tradeIndex, ⅓·estateTotal) form that §4.2.3 explicitly rejects as non-computable (estateTotal includes the trade term being clamped). new_trivial_fixes.where: prd.md §6.7.1 L6008 deriveDayKeyed; consumers §4.7.1 L4185, §4.2.2 L3443
new_trivial_fixes.fix: deriveDayKeyed is typed bare `number` with no range, but the weather/judged ±10% formulas (1 + 0.10·(2x−1)) only yield the intended band if x∈[0,1) — and splitmix64 (LOCKED) returns a 64-bit integer. Document deriveDayKeyed returns a normalized fraction ∈[0,1) (e.g. (splitmix64(...) mod 2^53)/2^53) once in §6.7.1 so the ±10% bands are well-defined and replay-stable. (Distinct from the round-2 cursor-seed BigInt-vs-number item.)

Reveal-on-load is unspecified: if prevState=∅ on cold boot, every already-unlocked surface diffs as 'new' → the whole reveal history re-animates and re-spams the log every refresh. Specify on load prevState = the loaded GameState (first render yields empty reveal diff), and gate oncePerGame log-line emission via the `unlocked` latch inside reduce/tick (not the renderer diff). Add a load(save)→render(state,state) zero-animation/zero-new-line test.

Log DATA is capped but the DOM-node lifecycle is unspecified — an append-on-new-line feed keeps every line's node forever (playtime-unbounded, tens of thousands of nodes over 60h, distinct from the bounded ~9-nav-tab note). Specify the log renderer reconciles by keyed id and removes/virtualizes DOM nodes for evicted ring entries; add a live-log-node-count ≤ ring-cap budget to the M6 perf check.

new_trivial_fixes.where: prd.md §6.9 L6165 K/M/B formatter in src/ui/
new_trivial_fixes.fix: The K/M/B display formatter (classic 999,999→'1000.0K' boundary bugs; resource counts now UNBOUNDED) is pure logic placed in the untested renderer layer outside `npm run verify`. Move it (and pure display helpers) into core/format under verify with table-driven boundary tests; the renderer imports it.

The bot — the instrument under every deterministic gate — has no determinism contract: 'highest-value deed' has no tie-break; the 'casual (sub-optimal) bot' needs RNG but none is specified; the value fn's float association order is unfixed. Give it a total stable action-id tie-break, a dedicated seed-pinned bot-RNG stream SEPARATE from the game cursors (casual variant), and an integer/rational value fn with fixed reduction order; commit bot seeds alongside the win-rate seed-set.

new_trivial_fixes.where: qa-playtesting.md §1 L81 / §6.10 forceState L6196 vs M6 harness L6866
new_trivial_fixes.fix: Time-compression helpers (toRung/toTier/forceState) fabricate/zero the per-rung tick-counts and let a win-rate be measured on a loadout that never paid its deed cost, while the gate still reports green. Add an invariant: the M6 pacing+fun+win-rate GATE runs are a single from-newGame(seed) full-play with skip-helpers DISABLED (sealed mode that throws), or telemetry tagged invalid if any jump occurred.

The reduced-motion backstop zeroes CSS animation-duration but the count-up/number-jump and '+N mote' are JS rAF tweens unaffected by it; and two motion authorities (OS prefers-reduced-motion + in-game A11ySettings.reduceMotion) have no single guard. Define selectReduceMotion(state)=state.settings.reducedMotion || matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce)').matches and have the JS tweens early-return to the final value when true.

new_human_questions.q: WCAG-AA contrast reconciliation (palette/hierarchy ADR). The PRD asserts 'passes AA on every paper surface' (§6.9/§6.11/§2.21) and 'AA-contrast' panels (§2.21d), and declares `--ink-faint` (#7A6C59) 'decorative-only / never carries a value'. But ui-design.md — the human-owned bible — routes information through ink-faint in 4 value-bearing roles (the system ARIA-live log §5.1; the next-rung requirement §5.4; captions/locked-hints §2; disabled-verb labels §5.5), all computing 4.23/3.66/3.31 (fail AA-normal); and identity-hue LABEL text fails too (家産 ochre 2.12, house-influence shu 3.23, kihada tags 1.42, the §5.1 coloured win/loss WORD 3.23/4.42). Resolve: promote informational ink-faint/hue uses to --ink-soft (loses the dim=de-emphasized + coloured-label scannable identity), demote hues to non-text fills/accents backed by an AA ink glyph, or darken the tokens? NOTE the baseline is fine: --ink-soft (8.49/7.34/6.64) and indigo key-number --ai (7.70/6.66/6.02) are confirmed AA-clean.

Win-rate gate methodology. The LOCKED 20-35% R3 band is wired as a HARD M6 build gate (M6 phase-4) but is a sampled probability over the combat cursor with NO N, no fixed seed-set, no tolerance/confidence policy, and the M2a DoD (L6628) conflates one-seed byte-identical replay with the many-seed rate. At a naive N≈20 the 95% CI is ≈[0.08,0.47], far wider than the 15-pt band → flaky/under-powered. Commit a fixed seed-set (N≥~500) and pick the pass rule: point-estimate-in-band (loose/stable) vs CI-fully-in-band (statistically demanding for a 15-pt human-LOCKED window)? Separate the determinism test (1 seed) from the win-rate test (sweep).

Pacing instrument validity. The ≥30-min/rung floor and the 4.5/8/16h tier budgets are tick-count × SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN — itself a free lever — not measured real dwell; a real-time-blind bot (ticks instantly) cannot validate that ~6000 ticks ≈ 34 real human minutes, and a single mis-set lever silently rescales the ENTIRE minutes-denominated acceptance battery (floor, tier budgets, AND the fun-proxy dead-time/cadence thresholds) while every undershoot gate still passes — a single point of failure the harness can't detect. Plus active-only+FU23 means even a real human's '30 min' is satisfiable by idle auto-advance. Accept the binding as a designer constant with a periodic HUMAN-session calibration checkpoint (measure real ticks/min, feed back SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN), or re-express the floor/budgets natively in ticks — and drop the 'hard data, not vibes' framing?

Estate dent vs the HARD trade-≤⅓ canon invariant. §4.2.4 dents the Estate pillar `value` scalar (−10%) while the land/treasury/trade sub-engines stay full, so (1) value desyncs from land+treasury+trade and (2) trade transiently exceeds ⅓ of the dented pillar value for the ~1-2 seasons the dent self-heals, corrupting the §6.6 proof on next load. The §6.6 verifier proves trade-≤⅓ post-COMBO (L5957) but has no post-DENT case and is build-time-only. Decide how a pillar-level dent maps onto the strands (proportional ×0.9 / single named strand + immediate re-clamp / make value purely derived =sum(strands)) and whether Estate.value is authoritative or derived; add a post-dent trade-≤⅓ assertion + a value==land+treasury+trade invariant. Also reconcile the M0-task circular tradeAllowed=⅓·estateTotal form (L6704) with §4.2.3's 0.5·(land+treasury).

Unattended auto-resolve COMBAT policy (ADR parallel to FU23). Retreat is a MANUAL mid-fight intervention (§2.8 L1490), so a left-running session at a fresh combat rung (fresh win-rate 20-45%, i.e. 55-80% LOSS) loss-spirals: each loss = 1 HP + ~½-day clock + a light injury, satiety drains (no auto-eat specified) → the satiety throttle lowers win-rate further. This inverts the relaxing 'leave it running, check progress' intent and can overshoot the §4.8 pacing measurement via repeated ½-day setbacks. Define an auto-engage threshold (predicted win ≥ X), auto-retreat (on predicted loss / satietyFrac < ~0.7), and the auto-eat/auto-rest behavior between auto-fights; assert in the M3 fun-proxy.

B5 combo-model resolution (refines the logged B5). Pick Model-A ('combo splits into BOTH paired pillars') EXPLICITLY and rewrite §6.5/§6.6/§6.6.1 — the four authored §4.3.1 pairs include Arms×Office & Estate×Office (no Name), so the 'combos land on a third pillar (Name)' wording (confirmed verbatim at §6.6 L5905) is structurally incoherent and Model-B cannot be the implementation. Separately the gate-EXCLUSION rule is unrepresentable (§6.4 stores no deed-vs-combo provenance) — add a deed-only `gateEligibleValue` accumulator per pillar that combos do NOT write (gate reads it, display reads `value`).

Active-combat engine. §4.6 models only stance + retreat; the named ability + item 'intervention slots' (a scheduled M2b reveal beat at weapon-L10) and ANY status-effect/initiative subsystem (which the weapon signature abilities — slow/first-strike/counter-on-evade/defense-shred — require) have ZERO mechanical model. Build a deterministic ability/item/status sub-model (trigger/cost/cooldown in sub-ticks, effect, duration, per-combatant status list on the combat cursor), or descope v1 active combat to stance+retreat and move the ability/item reveal beat + signature abilities post-v1?

Headless reveal-engine coverage + multi-threshold UX (refines the logged renderer-outside-verify question). Adopt a pure planReveals(state,prevState):RevealPlan factored out of the renderer and verify-gated (assert surfacesHiddenNow always empty [monotonicity], load idempotency planReveals(loaded,loaded)=∅, ≤1 reveal/frame or flag multiRevealFrames, deterministic in the replay suite). This needs a §6.9 contract revision + a §6.2 core module + a §6.6.1 invariant AND a UX decision: what happens when one atomic tick/load crosses N unlock thresholds (serialize over N frames / cap-per-frame / dump), plus the whitelist for legitimate multi-element reveals (the four-bar Influence panel).

Crafting quality formula is dimensionally broken AND coin is a dead v1 currency. (1) §4.7.2 outputQualityTier weights sum to 1.0 (a weighted average) then ÷ QUALITY_DIVISOR=10 → masterwork tiers unreachable in v1 and component/station inputs nearly inert, killing the silk meibutsu economic payoff + the Name-feed; re-derive QUALITY_DIVISOR and put crafterSkill/component/station on a common 0-5 scale. (2) Coin (mon) buys nothing concrete in v1 (every sink is koku+materials) yet MarketState/market-saturation is the ONLY persisted world-sim and its anti-runaway rationale is redundant with the ≤⅓ cap — give coin a concrete v1 sink or defer MarketState persistence + the damper to M4; and add ≥1 T1 estate-build koku sink (E1/E2 are T0, E3 is T2-G4 → ~8-16h T1 dead stretch with held koku piling up unspent).

Domain banzuke rival data. The §2.18 banzuke ranks houses by computedRankFromPillars and v1 surfaces a domain banzuke (G7 explicitly shows 'the Kurosawa leading' past Tomita/Akagi), but rival houses have NO numeric pillar values/trajectories anywhere — computedRankFromPillars is uncomputable for non-player houses and the 'contested meters [that] flip your way' (G3) have no model. Author static rival pillar/rank trajectories (Tomita money-led, Akagi precedence-led) + a small contested-meter model, or make non-player banzuke rows scripted-static milestones (drop computedRankFromPillars for them)?

new_strengths: Contrast baseline is genuinely backed by computation (refutes any suspicion 'passes AA' is unbacked for its scope): --ink-soft (#4A3F33) computes 8.49/7.34/6.64 and the indigo key-number --ai (#27496D) 7.70/6.66/6.02 — all clear WCAG AA-normal (4.5:1) with margin on all three paper surfaces. The warm-paper/warm-ink anti-#000-on-#FFF discipline costs no legibility; the contrast problems are confined to out-of-scope ink-faint value-roles and identity-hue label text, NOT the ink-soft/ai baseline (which should be the locked passing rows of a contrast gate).

The derived-not-stored discipline (§6.4 L5751: 'store only what is NOT derivable') structurally neutralizes the largest class of 60h save/memory bloat — weather, lunar, satietyMax, effectiveStats, production rates, unlocked-set, tier progress are all RECOMPUTED, and deriveDayKeyed(seed,channel,day) is stateless/day-keyed so no per-day cursor array grows. This is the right architecture for a leave-it-running game and is exactly why the residual hazards reduce to a short fixable list (persisted log, unpruned effects, per-tick allocation, log-DOM lifecycle) rather than a pervasive problem.

The QA harness skeleton is the right instrument architecture: it drives the SAME typed reduce/tick intents a player triggers (no synthetic input, no test-only paths), the single seeded RNG makes a fixed (seed, intent-script) byte-reproducible, and the fun-proxies land report-only at M1/M3 and are promoted to a hard gate only at M6 after thresholds are tuned (sound risk ordering). The round-3 findings are gaps in the bot's POLICY, statistical protocol, and determinism contract — all addable without reshaping the architecture.

The tick↔day↔season↔wall-clock primitive under ALL pacing is well-defined (§4.0 L3213-3223: 200 ticks/day, 6000/season, 4 seasons/year, SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN[tier] committed as an explicit lever) and ties out (T0 ~8 seasons × ~34 min ≈ 270 min = the 4.5h floor); attributes are wired into combat math (§4.4/§4.6.1, not dead data) — refuting a prospective 'pacing primitive / attribute-math unpinned' completeness gap. Only the implied ~2.9 ticks/s active-play rate is worth a sanity check at M1 profiling.
refutations: B4 REFUTED as an independent 4th BLOCKER → downgrade to a §4.1.1 prose fix + fold the residual into the already-logged Q4/Q1. Round-3 evidence: (1) the koku column is NOT meter-gated — koku is labour-produced at the throughput rate over elapsed wall-clock (R7: 150/min × 55 = 8.25K = the exact table figure), so the '30·500=15.0K collapse' horn is false; (2) promotion is an AND-gate (rungMeter ≥ threshold AND requiredStoryFlags, L3385), so the meter filling at 30 min does NOT release the rung while a story flag lags — the meter is a per-rung FLOOR enforcer, not the sole gate; (3) §6.6.1 asserts threshold reachability+monotonicity, never 'meter-fill-time == wall-clock', so it is neither failing nor vacuous. The TRUE residual = the §4.1.1 prose claim that the meter and wall-clock 'land together' is false for R4-R7 (meter lands at the 30-min floor; the wall-clock/koku run longer) — exactly Q4 (floor-enforcement) + Q1 (pace-ceiling), already logged, not a new structural break.

B1 KEEP but framing CORRECTED: the 'stale 140K ref' wording is wrong — TIER_REF_NAME = armsGood 30K + estateGood 60K + officeGood 50K = 140K is the CORRECT current sum of the T2 good bands (verified verbatim at §4.2.2). The real bug is the CHOICE of reference: JUDGE_K[Name][T2] = 0.30·140K = 42,000 = Name's GREAT band, so the defect is WORSE than 'auto-good' — at the T2 gate (which forces Estate/Office to great/excellent → highWater sum ≥ 192K > 140K → clamp=1.0 → f_Name=1.0) Name auto-reaches GREAT with ZERO Name deeds. '(Name & Honour) gates no v1 tier' sits literally inside the JUDGE_K derivation while §4.1 lists Name with a 28K good band in the T2 end-gate — the §4.1↔§4.2.2 contradiction is the open Q6; one must be authoritative.

B5 KEEP but NARROWED to Model-A: §4.3.1's four authored combo pairs are Arms×Office, Estate×Office, Estate×Name, Arms×Name — TWO contain no Name, so §6.5/§6.6/§6.6.1's 'combos land on a third pillar (Name)' (confirmed verbatim at §6.6 L5905) is structurally incoherent and Model-B cannot be the implementation. So §6.5/§6.6/§6.6.1 must be rewritten to Model-A ('both paired pillars'). The gate-EXCLUSION sub-defect stands: §6.4 stores no deed-vs-combo provenance, so the M5/M6 'gate-excluded' assertions have no storage hook → needs a deed-only gateEligibleValue accumulator.

B3 KEEP but SCOPED precisely: import-over is NOT wholly unhandled — §6.8 L6044 + §6.8.2 L6122 + Q19 make it an explicit destructive-CONFIRM with an automatic pre-overwrite snapshot. The two precise residual gaps: (1) the imported envelope's saveCounter is never rebased above local-max, so a typically-lower-counter friend/QA/old-self file can be re-elected away by the higher-counter pre-overwrite snapshot via newest-wins; (2) additive optional fields WITHOUT a schemaVersion bump (§6.4 L5804) defeat the forward-version guard (fires only on schemaVersion mismatch) → a same-version stale-CDN build silently truncates the unknown field on re-save. Fix: rebase saveCounter=localMax+1 + keep the snapshot in a recovery key the selector never reads + bump schemaVersion (or mandate preserve-unknown-fields) on every additive field.

Prospective 'day-length / wall-clock binding unpinned' completeness gap REFUTED: §4.0 explicitly binds tick↔day↔season and commits SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN[tier] (T0 ~34 / T1 ~60 / T2 ~120 min) and it ties out (T0 ~8 seasons × ~34 min ≈ 270 min = the 4.5h floor); separately attributes are wired into all combat derived stats (§4.4/§4.6.1), refuting an 'attributes are dead data' suspicion. Both pacing-primitive and attribute-math are well-specified.

B2 / B6 / B7 KEPT and confirmed/strengthened, NOT refuted: B2 sharpened with hard new evidence — the gate field is literally `pillarFloor` (§2.16) so it reads influence[pillar].value (Phase-2-frozen), and E3@G4 needs Estate≥40K/Arms≥15K vs pillars frozen at end-T1 (~15K/~10K), with §4.7.5's affordability reconciliation comparing the WRONG reference (end-T2 good-bands 60K/30K, reached only in T2 Phase 2, long after the G4 check). B6 = a clean three-way §2.2-vs-§6.4-vs-§2.19 store-vs-derive contradiction (+ lunar-from-hash teleport). B7 = a full grep for navigator.locks/BroadcastChannel/storage-event/leader-election/instanceId across all three binding docs returns ZERO hits — zero concurrency primitives, so the scalar saveCounter provably cannot detect concurrent multi-tab divergence.
synthesis.summary: VERDICT: NOT-DRY, but CONVERGING toward dry on the blocking spine. Round 3 drove a deep completeness pass into the one quadrant rounds 1-2 under-covered — the second-order economy/crafting/combat-depth/display subsystems plus the a11y, perf-memory, and QA-bot-rigor lenses — and it WAS productive, so by the project's saturation rule (two consecutive rounds adding no new actionable findings) the battery must continue. It surfaced ~34 genuinely-new actionable items, including ONE new CRITICAL blocker (B8: no costed/itemized/time-budgeted/schema-representable supply for the great/excellent gate bands — every v1 tier-gate is unclearable-as-costed and the §6.6.1 verifier asserts two mutually-exclusive things; I confirmed this verbatim against §4.1/§4.2.1/§4.2.2/§6.6.1). B8 lives in the SAME accrual-machinery family as B1/B4/B5 and is plausibly the last big one in that vein. Beyond B8 the signal shifted decisively to high/medium DESIGN-CALL and SPEC-COMPLETENESS material rather than structural breaks: five high needs-human items (the WCAG-AA contrast contradiction between the PRD and the human-owned ui-design.md bible — ink-faint value-roles + identity-hue label text fail AA while ink-soft/ai are confirmed AA-clean; the win-rate gate has no sampling protocol and conflates 1-seed-determinism with many-seed-rate; the ≥30-min floor measures ticks×a-lever not real dwell so a single mis-set lever silently rescales the whole minutes-battery; a scripted Estate dent transiently breaks the HARD trade-≤⅓ invariant via value/strand desync; and an unattended auto-resolve combat loss-spiral inverts FU23), plus a cluster of trivial-fix spec holes (unquantified log cap, no effects-prune rule, doubly-specified reveal authority, FU4 batched-tick, reveal-on-load re-spam, circular Estate basis, deriveDayKeyed range, bot determinism, telemetry contamination) and medium economy/combat-depth gaps (dimensionally-broken crafting formula, unmodeled ability/item/status combat engine, banzuke rival data, dead coin currency). REFUTATIONS: B4 is refuted as an independent 4th blocker (koku is throughput-not-meter-gated; AND-gate; verifier checks reachability not fill-time) and folds into the logged Q4/Q1; B1's 'stale 140K' framing is corrected (140K is the correct current sum; the bug is the reference choice and Name auto-reaches GREAT, not merely good); B5 narrows to 'Model-A is correct, rewrite §6.5/§6.6/§6.6.1'; B3 scopes to two precise gaps (saveCounter-rebase + same-version field truncation); and a prospective 'wall-clock-binding unpinned' gap is refuted (§4.0 binds and ties out). B2/B6/B7 are kept and strengthened. Three strengths reaffirmed: the AA baseline (ink-soft/ai), the derived-not-stored discipline, and the QA harness skeleton. RECOMMENDATION: resolve B8 + ship the new high human-questions to the human glance alongside B1-B3/B5-B7, then run ONE more targeted round scoped narrowly to economy/crafting (§4.7.2/§2.4), reputation-multiplier (§2.15), banzuke (§2.18), and the active-combat engine (§4.6); if that round yields only low-severity/proposed-number findings, declare DRY. The locked-intent spine, balance tie-outs, determinism/save model and NFR surface are converging — the residue is largely the provisional plan layer D-021 keeps liquid by design."

confirmed.title: `--ink-faint` is declared "decorative-only, never carries a value" but the UI bible assigns it four value-bearing roles — all of which FAIL WCAG AA (computed 4.23 / 3.66 / 3.31)
confirmed.where: prd.md §6.11 L6224, §2.21 L2353, §6.9 L6164 + §7 M6-task7 L6872 (all say ink-faint decorative-only) vs ui-design.md §2 L46, §5.1 system-channel L322, §5.4 L360, §5.5 L369
confirmed.detail: The PRD's contrast escape-hatch is: ink-soft is the functional-text colour (passes AA) and `--ink-faint` (#7A6C59) "is decorative-only (never carries a value)". The ui-design.md bible flatly contradicts this and routes information through ink-faint in four places: (1) §2 token comment defines it as "muted labels, locked hints, captions"; (2) §5.1 the `system` log channel (restocks, saves) is `--ink-faint`, "smaller" — and that log is an ARIA live region, i.e. functional text; (3) §5.4 the next rung renders as "`--ink-faint` silhouette WITH ITS REQUIREMENT" — the unlock requirement a player must read; (4) §5.5 an unavailable verb's label is `--ink-faint` ("faded"). Computed WCAG ratios for #7A6C59: on --washi(#F3E9D2)=4.23, on --washi-shade(#E7D9BC)=3.66, on --washi-deep(#DDCFAE)=3.31. All three are below the 4.5:1 AA-normal floor; they only clear AA-large (3:1), but these roles are captions/labels/pips at --fs-small(14px) and --fs-micro(12px) (§3.3 L165-166), which are NOT WCAG large text (large = 24px / 18.66px-bold). So every ink-faint value fails AA, and on the recessed washi-deep surface (3.31) the next-rung requirement is barely above the large floor it doesn't even qualify for. The 'decorative-only' claim is not honoured by the only document that maps tokens to roles.
confirmed.fix: Reconcile the two docs: either (a) promote every value-bearing ink-faint use — next-rung requirement, locked-item hint/unlock condition, system-log line, disabled-verb label — to `--ink-soft` (which clears AA, see the positive finding), leaving ink-faint ONLY on truly non-informative chrome (e.g. the §4.3 inner keyline border, which is non-text); or (b) darken `--ink-faint` until it clears 4.5:1 on washi-deep (needs ≈#6A5C49 or darker). Then make the contrast gate (separate finding) assert ink-faint's chosen role-set, so the contradiction can't reappear.
confirmed.verdict: REAL and reproducible. PRD declares `--ink-faint` (#7A6C59) "decorative-only (never carries a value)" at all 4 cited spots (§6.11 L6224, §2.21 L2353, §6.9 L6164, §7 M6-task7 L6872), and §6.9 L6161 cross-refs ui-design.md as the styling authority. ui-design.md — the human-owned UI bible — contradicts this by routing information through ink-faint in 4 places: §2 L46 token comment ("muted labels, locked hints, captions"); §5.1 L321 system log channel (an ARIA live region per L312 — functional text: restocks/saves); §5.4 L360-361 next-rung silhouette "with its requirement" (planning text); §5.5 L368-369 unavailable verb label ("faded"). I independently computed the WCAG ratios for #7A6C59 and they reproduce EXACTLY: on --washi 4.23, --washi-shade 3.66, --washi-deep 3.31 — all below the 4.5:1 AA-normal floor, clearing only AA-large (3:1). The roles render at --fs-small 14px / --fs-micro 12px (§3.3 L165-166) and even --fs-body is 16px — none is WCAG large text (24px / 18.66px-bold), so none earns the 3:1 allowance. By contrast --ink-soft #4A3F33 computes 8.49/7.34/6.64 (AA-clean), confirming the escape-hatch logic. GENUINELY NEW: the only prior a11y ledger entry (L120) is about --ink-soft's unverified "AA on every surface" claim + the missing gate; this is a different token, a direct PRD-vs-bible contradiction, with computed failing ratios. One fair caveat (does not defeat it): WCAG 1.4.3 exempts inactive components, so role #4 (disabled/"faded" button label) may be contrast-exempt — but it still carries a value, so still falsifies "never carries a value"; roles #1 (captions/labels), #2 (the live-region system log — no exemption), #3 (next-rung requirement) are not inactive and cleanly fail AA. DISPOSITION needs-human: the PRD's "never carries a value" sentence is factually wrong and must be reconciled, but the fix is a design-language/taste call the human owns. Options trade off — (a) move the informational uses to --ink-soft, erasing the deliberate dim=de-emphasized/locked/unavailable/quiet hierarchy (§5.1 "quietest tier", §5.4 silhouette, §5.5 faded); (b) darken --ink-faint to pass AA, mutating a core INK palette token and compressing the ink/ink-soft/ink-faint three-step contrast ladder of the woodblock low-contrast aesthetic; or (c) restrict ink-faint to genuinely-inactive/decorative cases and re-route system-log + next-rung-requirement + captions to --ink-soft. Each is a palette/hierarchy decision warranting an ADR, not a mechanical find-replace.

confirmed.title: "Passes WCAG AA on every paper surface" is scoped only to ink text — the many COLOURED text/label roles (four-pillar kanji, category tags, win-word) compute to 1.42–3.73 and fail; the signature 家産 estate label in ochre = 2.12
confirmed.where: ui-design.md §2 palette L60-63 & L81-85, §5.1 combat-channel L320, §5.3 four-pillar table L345-350 vs prd.md §6.9 L6163 / §6.11 L6223 / §2.21 L2352 ("passes WCAG AA on every paper surface")
confirmed.detail: The AA assertion is carefully worded to cover only `--ink-soft` functional text — but the design renders meaning in non-ink hues that the claim silently excludes. Computed on the panel surface --washi-shade(#E7D9BC): four-pillar KANJI label rendered in its pillar 'own ink' (§2 L80 "each pillar gets its OWN ink"; §5.3 L345 "kanji label + emoji + pillar colour") — pillar-estate=ochre(#C68A3E)=2.12 FAIL, pillar-kai/家威 roll-up=shu(#D7402C)=3.23 (large-only), pillar-arms=beni=4.42 (large-only), pillar-name=rokusho=4.47 (large-only); only pillar-office=ai=6.66 clears AA. Category tags / status pips (§2 L60-63, --fs-micro 12px): kihada(#E0B23C)=1.42, ochre=2.12, gold(#B08D4F)=2.22 — kihada at 1.42:1 is effectively illegible as text. Combat-channel outcome WORD coloured --shu win (§5.1 L320): shu on washi-shade=3.23, on washi-deep=2.92 (fails even large). --ai-soft used for 'muted fills/bars' (§2 L50)=2.70 on washi-shade. So the signature four-pillar panel — the §10 House/Influence capstone — has its estate label at 2.12:1 and its house-influence roll-up at 3.23:1. Emoji+kanji carry redundant meaning so this isn't a colour-ONLY-encoding failure, but the TEXT LEGIBILITY of these coloured labels fails AA, which the headline claim conceals by only ever naming ink-soft.
confirmed.fix: Either render all pillar kanji / tag / outcome labels in `--ink`/`--ink-soft` and use the pillar/tag hue ONLY as a non-text accent (bar fill, pip swatch, underline) — keeping colour redundant to an ink glyph — or restrict the AA claim's wording to 'ink text' and add a separate, honest line that hue-on-paper labels are decorative accents backed by an AA-contrast ink glyph. Add these fg/bg pairings to the contrast gate's table so a future palette tweak can't silently drop a label below 4.5.
confirmed.verdict:
REAL, genuinely NEW (no contrast/WCAG-AA finding in the rounds 1-2 known/open list; "a11y-no-buildable-gate" and "legibility-codex" are different). Verified, math reproduces EXACTLY.

CONTRAST MATH (WCAG 2.x formula, foreground on panel surface --washi-shade #E7D9BC): ochre #C68A3E=2.12; shu #D7402C=3.23 (washi-deep #DDCFAE=2.92); beni #A93B47=4.42; rokusho #3E6958=4.47; ai #27496D=6.66; kihada #E0B23C=1.42; gold #B08D4F=2.22; ai-soft #5C8B93=2.70. Every number in the claim is exact. Control: --ink-soft #4A3F33=7.34, so the headline ink-soft claim is itself true.

TEXT VERIFIED: The WCAG AA assertion is verbatim scoped to functional/hint text=`--ink-soft` at all three sites — prd.md:6163, prd.md:6223, prd.md:2352 (2352 reads "all paper surfaces"; 6163/6223 read "every paper surface"). The palette hexes/roles are at ui-design.md:60-63, :81-85; the four-pillar table at :344-350.

The single AIRTIGHT defect: ui-design.md:320 (§5.1, the HERO log) explicitly renders a WORD in colour — "outcome word coloured `--shu` (win) / `--beni` (loss)". That is meaning-bearing TEXT, not a fill, at 3.23 (win/shu) and 4.42 (loss/beni) on the log surface — both below 4.5, so WCAG 1.4.3 (contrast-minimum) fails for normal text (pass only if rendered as large text, which body log lines are not). Second solid defect: ui-design.md:60 `--kihada`="category tags, status pips" at `--fs-micro` 12px = 1.42 — effectively illegible as text. Note WCAG 1.4.1 (colour-not-sole-carrier) IS satisfied (claim concedes redundancy); the live failure is 1.4.3, which redundancy does not rescue.

OVERCLAIM the claim under-cites: prd.md:2365 (§2.21d) asserts the pillar/standing panels are rendered "AA-contrast" — stronger than the scoped ink-soft line and directly overstated if any pillar-hued label text is used.

REFUTATIONS / caveats (kept the claim honest): (1) The specific 家産-ochre=2.12 KANJI-LABEL is INFERRED — that labels render in raw pillar hue rests on §2:80 "each pillar gets its OWN ink" + §5.3:341 "kanji label + emoji + pillar colour"; not airtight, since the hue could be a fill/accent and fills get the explicit "meter fill is darkened for contrast" escape (prd.md:6164/6224). (2) ai-soft 2.70 is listed but L50 scopes it to "muted fills/bars" (a fill, not text) — weak sub-point. (3) Headline range "1.42-3.73" is loose; the fail-as-normal set actually spans 1.42-4.47. (4) The PRD never states the coloured roles pass AA, so the "concealment/contradiction" framing is rhetorical — only L2365 is a true overclaim; the combat word and category tags are simply unaddressed by any AA claim.

DISPOSITION = needs-human (not trivial-fix). The two overstated lines (prd.md:2365 and the headline scoping) are one-line edits, BUT they sit on top of a real palette-vs-AA tension that cannot be edited away: the signature woodblock earth hues (ochre/kihada/gold/shu) on cream paper are intrinsically low-contrast, and §2.16/§5.3 deliberately use coloured LABELS as the "peripherally scannable, each-pillar-its-own-ink" identity device (ui-design.md:80,99-100), while §5.1:320 puts a coloured WORD on the hero log as a juice beat. Making these AA-conformant forces a design tradeoff — darken the brand hues (the seal red, ochre) and lose their identity, OR demote colour to fill/accent-only and render all label text in ink (losing the scannable coloured-label device and the red "WIN" punch), OR formally accept them as decorative-redundant and add an ink-rendered accessible duplicate (a component change). Per CLAUDE.md the human is "the final fun & taste arbiter" and fun-vs-accessibility/visual calls route to a human + an ADR. Recommend logging as an open H-item: reconcile prd.md:2365 + the headline ink-soft scoping with §5.1:320 / §2.16 coloured-label intent, and add an explicit rule for whether identity hues may carry TEXT (and at what darkened AA-passing values) vs fills/accents only.

confirmed.title: The persisted event log is the ONLY playtime-scaling save component, its cap is unquantified, and it is written redundantly to 3 backends each autosave — directly contradicting the "save tiny" claim and risking the R3 quota-fail banner over 60h
confirmed.where: §6.4 L5789 `log: LogEntry[]; // capped`; §6.4 module table L5669 `core/log` = "append, cap, severity/colour tag"; §2.1 L1182 "stored in a capped ring buffer"; §6.8 L6052 persists "the capped event log"; §6.8.1 L6083-6086 (write same envelope to IndexedDB+localStorage+sessionStorage); contra §6.4 L5823 "keeps the save tiny"
confirmed.detail: Every other stored field is bounded by CONTENT cardinality (resources/skills/quests/flags/unlocked/counts/reputation are all keyed by a finite authored id set), so they cannot grow with playtime. The `log` is the sole exception: it accrues with every reveal, narration, reward, and — critically — every auto-resolve combat line, and it IS in the persisted surface (§6.8 L6052). Its cap is described as "capped" / "append, cap" in THREE places but a number appears NOWHERE. Computation: a LogEntry `{id,text,channel,colorClass,tick}` serializes to ~100–150 bytes. Whatever a dev picks for the unspecified cap dominates the save — even a modest 1,000-entry ring = ~120 KB, making the log the single LARGEST save component and falsifying "save tiny." Worse, during continuous FU23 auto-resolve/auto-repeat grinding the ring is pinned at MAX size for hours, so the save always carries the max-size log. That blob is then JSON-stringified into localStorage AND sessionStorage and structured-cloned into IDB on every autosave (§6.8 L6031: debounced + on a tick interval + visibilitychange/beforeunload), and base64-export inflates it +33%. Under itch's cross-origin-iframe storage partition (smaller/evictable quota, the named R3 threat), an over-generous log cap is a realistic path to the "couldn't save — export a backup" banner mid-session.
confirmed.fix: Pin a hard LOG_RING_MAX as a balance constant (propose ~300 entries) enforced in `core/log` on push (true ring eviction), and DECOUPLE display history from persistence: persist only the last ~50 lines (or omit the log from the save entirely — it is cosmetic history that nothing recomputes from). Add a §6.6 verifier assert that the stored envelope size stays under an M0/M1 save-size budget (propose JSON envelope ≤ 64 KB typical / ≤ 256 KB hard ceiling, an order of magnitude under the ~5 MB localStorage quota and the partitioned-iframe headroom). Enforce at the same M0/M1 profiling step R7/Q56 names.
confirmed.verdict:
REAL as a spec gap; the alarming causal framing is OVERSTATED. CONFIRMED from the text: (1) the event-log cap is genuinely unquantified — "capped"/"ring buffer"/"append, cap" appear at L1149, L1182, L2598, §6.4 module-table L5669, §6.4 field L5789, §6.8 persisted-surface L6052, M0-DoD L6545, but NO cap NUMBER appears anywhere in the PRD; (2) `log: LogEntry[]` is the unique playtime-scaling save field — every other stored field (L5765-5790) is bounded by content cardinality (keyed by a finite authored id set), and the only other arrays self-bound (`effects[]` self-prunes on duration expiry; `combat?` is one live encounter), so the log is the sole field that accrues monotonically with every reveal/narration/reward/auto-resolve combat line; (3) it IS in the persisted surface (§6.8 L6052), written redundantly to 3 backends (IndexedDB+localStorage+sessionStorage, §6.8.1 L6083-6086), plus a pre-overwrite snapshot write (L6065) and +33% on base64 export, against the explicit "keeps the save tiny" assertion (§6.4 L5823). So "save tiny" is UNVERIFIABLE for the one field whose size is a free parameter nobody pinned. This is NEW vs rounds 1-2 (B3/B7 concern save selection/concurrency, not size; the ledger has zero log-cap/save-size/quota mentions).

REFUTED part of the claim: "playtime-scaling … over 60h … realistic R3 quota-fail banner." A ring buffer is CONSTANT-size once full — it does not grow with playtime; size is fixed by the CAP CHOICE, not by hours played, and is pinned-at-max from whenever it first fills (early), so the "over 60h" runaway/time-dependent framing does not reproduce. The quota-fail path is realistic only under a pathologically large cap (tens of thousands of entries → MBs); a sane few-hundred-entry ring (~30-75 KB) sits trivially inside even itch's partitioned/evictable quota. So the headline threat is a STATIC save-size-budget gap, not a runaway/quota failure.

DISPOSITION trivial-fix (not needs-human, unlike B1-B7): the remedy is a sensible-default spec addition — name a default LOG_CAP (e.g. ~300 entries) in §6.4/§2.1/core/log, and ideally specify that the PERSISTED log is a small tail (e.g. last 50-100 lines) decoupled from the larger in-memory display ring so the save stays demonstrably tiny. The only judgment (scrollback depth vs save size) has an obvious safe default, so per the project's bias-to-action ethos a dev picks the number and moves on; no human design call required. Suggest logging as a trivial-fix in the round-3 ledger (with the "save-size budget / persisted-tail" note), NOT as a new blocking defect.

confirmed.title: `effects: ActiveEffect[]` has no stated prune-on-expiry rule — stale buffs/injuries accumulate unbounded in both runtime state and the persisted save over a buff-heavy 60h session, and are re-summed every effective-stats compute
confirmed.where: §6.4 L5787 `effects: ActiveEffect[]; // active buffs/injuries with remaining duration`; persisted at §6.8 L6053 "active-effect remainders"; the per-tick step §6.3 L5719-5723 lists satiety drain / activity progress / producer output but NO effect-expiry filter; FoodItem buffs are frequent by design (§2.3 L1258, "eat before you fight")
confirmed.detail: The array is named "ACTIVE buffs/injuries with remaining duration," but nothing in the tick contract says expired effects are removed. If `tick` only DECREMENTS `remainingTicks` (or appends new effects) without FILTERING expired ones, the array grows monotonically. Computation: over 60h (~3,600 in-game-min) with the satiety/eat-before-you-fight loop applying a food buff every few minutes, ~1,000+ stale effects accumulate — ~80 KB of dead state in `effects` AND in every autosave, plus a compounding CPU cost: `effectiveStats(state)` (§6.4 L5814) sums over `effects` on every render and every combat-power read, so an unbounded `effects` makes a per-frame O(n) selector grow without bound. This is both a memory leak and a tick-loop allocation/iteration hazard, and it is invisible to the content-cardinality reasoning that bounds every other field.
confirmed.fix: Make `tick` FILTER OUT effects whose remaining duration reached zero each tick (the array must hold only currently-active effects, length bounded by the count of simultaneously-stackable buffs/injuries — a small constant). Add a Vitest invariant + §6.6 assertion that `effects.length` stays under a small bound across a long scripted run, and fold it into the M0/M1 per-tick budget.
confirmed.verdict: REAL but severity-overstated; trivially fixable. CONFIRMED from text: (1) §6.4 L5787 `effects: ActiveEffect[] // active buffs/injuries with remaining duration` is a PERSISTED field (§6.8 L6053 "active-effect remainders"); (2) the §6.3 per-tick contract (L5719-5723) enumerates auto-combat / auto-labour / satiety drain / activity progress / producer output and is SILENT on effect-expiry — a full-PRD grep for expire|prune|decrement|remainingTicks|filter|tick-down finds NO prune-on-expiry rule anywhere; (3) the eat-before-you-fight buff loop is real (FoodItem.buffs?, L1258; phrase recurs L141/217/1238/2633). NEW, load-bearing observation not in rounds 1-2: `log` is explicitly a "capped ring buffer / capped event log" (L1182, L5789), and every other persisted collection (resources/inventory/counts/flags/unlocked/producers) is content-cardinality-bounded — `effects` is the ONLY persisted collection that is neither cardinality-bounded NOR explicitly capped, an asymmetry against the very-careful `log` cap. This is invisible to the round 1-2 content-cardinality reasoning. WHERE THE CLAIM OVERSTATES (refuted): effectiveStats at L5814 lists base+additive+multiplier+equipment+milestones+perks and does NOT actually name effects/buffs as a layer, so the "per-frame O(n) selector grows without bound" is inference, not text; "unbounded growth / 80KB leak / 1000+ stale effects" presumes a naive append-never-filter impl, whereas the field is literally named "with remaining duration" and content/effects.ts (L5885) encodes "duration, stacking" — an EffectId-keyed refresh stacking model would bound the array by content cardinality. So the memory-leak/CPU-hazard dramatization is overstated; the genuine gap is a spec-completeness hole. FIX is trivial: add one clause to the §6.3 per-tick bullet ("decrement remainingTicks; drop expired effects") and/or note `effects` is stacking-bounded or capped, mirroring the existing `log` cap. No design decision about what the game IS — not a human call.

confirmed.title: Reveal truth is doubly-specified — stored monotonic `unlocked` Set vs recomputed `unlockedSurfaces(state)`; non-monotonic predicates UN-reveal panels
confirmed.where: §6.4 GameState (line 5784 `unlocked: Set<SurfaceId>` = "the ONLY reveal state"; line 5796 "a surface flips when its predicate passes"; line 5818 `unlockedSurfaces(state)` listed as a COMPUTED selector, "never stored") vs §6.9 (6137 "renderer shows only `unlockedSurfaces(state)`", 6140 "shows what the predicates currently pass") vs §6.3 (5713 reduce "flips to unlocked"). Predicate inputs per §2.1 (1175-6).
confirmed.detail: The reveal source-of-truth is specified two incompatible ways and both are used as canon. (A) §6.4-stored + §6.3: a write-once monotonic latch — `reduce` flips a surface into the persisted `unlocked` Set when its predicate first passes; the Set is "the ONLY reveal state." (B) §6.4-selectors + §6.9: a pure derived recompute — the renderer "shows what the predicates currently pass," so `unlockedSurfaces(state)` is computed-never-stored. These disagree because RevealableEntry.unlockPredicate is explicitly allowed to read NON-MONOTONIC inputs: `season`, `resources`, `rank`, `pillar values` (§2.1 line 1175-6). All four FALL in normal play: season cycles spring→summer; "koku and coin are inputs you spend" (§2.4); pillars take recoverable DENTS (§4.2.4); and rung-meters are PER-RUNG-RESET (§6.4 line 5780, §1.5.1 line 266). Under reading (B), a panel whose predicate references any of these UN-reveals — vanishes when the season turns, when you spend below a resource threshold, on a pillar dent, or (worst) every time a rung-meter resets. That directly violates "reveals read as plot, never un-happen," the "no reset, ever" pillar (§1.2 / 6328), and the room-by-room "never un-reveal" promise. Under reading (A) the stored Set is correct but then §6.4 listing `unlockedSurfaces` as derived-never-stored and §6.9 "what predicates currently pass" are both wrong, and the Set is the thing that must be saved. The verifier's `unreachableReveals` (§2.20/§6.6.1) only checks a predicate CAN be satisfied — there is no invariant that a reveal predicate is monotonic or latched, so a season/resource/rung-gated reveal passes CI yet flickers in play.
confirmed.fix: Make the stored `unlocked` Set the single authority: `reduce`/`tick` ADD an id the first time its predicate passes and NEVER remove it; define `unlockedSurfaces(state)` as `state.unlocked` (optionally ∪ ids newly-passing this step), never a bare predicate re-eval. Fix §6.4-selectors and §6.9 prose to say "reads the latched Set," not "what predicates currently pass." Add a verifier invariant (`nonMonotonicRevealViolations`): a RevealableEntry predicate may reference falling inputs (season/resource/rank/pillar) only through the latch (reveal-once), and a property test asserts no surface in `unlocked` is ever removed across a tick/reduce sequence.
confirmed.verdict: REAL (new; not in rounds 1-2), but narrower than stated — a latent spec-ambiguity hazard, not a true 50/50 contradiction. CONFIRMED FROM TEXT: (1) the reveal source-of-truth is double-worded — §6.4/5784 "`unlocked: Set<SurfaceId>` = the ONLY reveal state" + §6.3/5713 "newly-earned surfaces FLIP to unlocked" (an edge-triggered persisted latch, reading A) coexists with §6.4/5818 listing `unlockedSurfaces(state)` under "Computed (never stored)" + §6.9/6137-6140 "renderer shows only `unlockedSurfaces(state)` ... shows what the predicates CURRENTLY pass" (a level-triggered recompute, reading B). (2) §2.1/1175 explicitly permits non-monotonic predicate inputs (season, resources, rank, pillar values); all four fall in normal play — season cycles (5728), "Koku and coin are inputs you spend" (1282), dents remove 0.10·value (§4.2.4/3703), rung-meters PER-RUNG-RESET (266/5780). (3) Under a literal reading (B) any reveal whose predicate references a falling input would UN-reveal — violating "a reveal reads as plot" (5891), "no reset, ever" (6328), "No system ever wipes" (2310). (4) CI cannot catch it: §6.6.1 (5943-5969) has reachability/monotonicity invariants for rung-meters and gates but NONE for reveal predicates; `unreachableReveals[]` (2318) checks satisfiability only — a season/resource/rung-meter-gated reveal passes the verifier yet flickers in play. BONUS (new): §6.2/5665 names the reveal selectors `isUnlocked`/`visibleSurfaces`, a third name vs §6.4/§6.9 `unlockedSurfaces` — corroborating that this read-surface is loosely pinned. ADVERSARIAL DISCOUNT: reading (A) is the DOMINANT canon, not co-equal — the field is "the ONLY reveal state," is persisted (a GameState field, so saved), all four authored trigger kinds (RANK/PILLAR/STORY/FIRST-USE, 2488-2495) are monotonic, and "FIRST-TIME reveal" (6140) presupposes latch memory. Reading (B) survives only on the reconcilable "computed projection" listing + one imprecise §6.9 phrase. No intended transient surface exists (seasonal "windows" attach to activity ELIGIBILITY 6581, not to the `unlocked` Set), so no design decision is buried here. DISPOSITION trivial-fix: the intent is unambiguous (monotonic latch), so no human "what is the game" call is needed. Cleanest fix is an ENGINE CONTRACT — state that `reduce`/`tick` only ever ADD to `unlocked` (write-once; never remove), so even a non-monotonic predicate latches on first pass and the renderer reads `state.unlocked`, never live predicates — plus reword §6.9 ("shows the surfaces in `state.unlocked`," not "what predicates currently pass"), pin `unlockedSurfaces(state)` as a pure projection of the stored Set in §6.4, unify the §6.2 selector name, and add a one-line §6.6.1 guard/test that a surface never leaves `unlocked` once entered. Multi-site but mechanically directed; no design ambiguity. Not blocking but should be fixed before M0 since `unlocked` is part of the persisted save surface.

confirmed.title: NO-UI-DUMPS guarantee (FU4) breaks under batched ticks — many reveals flip between two renders with no queue to serialize them
confirmed.where: §2.1 FU4 (1155-1162 "no two collide on a single tick"; "NO runtime revealQueue"), §6.4 NON-field callout (5794-5798), §6.3 tick (5704-5740 whole-integer dtTicks, auto-resolve/auto-repeat "run unattended for hours"), §6.9 (6169-6172), §6.10 (`tick(N)`, `frames(n)`, `toRung`, `toTier`, `advanceSeason`).
confirmed.detail: FU4's anti-dump claim is that the authored schedule spaces beats so "no two collide on a single TICK," and therefore no runtime queue is needed. But `tick(state, dtTicks)` advances MANY whole ticks per call and the renderer reconciles per FRAME, not per tick: the app loop computes dtTicks from elapsed active time and auto-resolve combat + auto-repeat labour "run unattended for hours" (§6.9 6171). Whenever a frame spans K ticks — a slow/janky frame, a backgrounded-then-foregrounded tab, or any QA fast-forward (`__qa.tick(N)`, `frames(n)`, `advanceSeason()`, `toRung()`, `toTier()`) — K reveal predicates can flip between two `render(state, prevState)` reconciliations. With no runtime queue (FU4 explicitly forbids `revealQueue`), `render` paints all K newly-unlocked surfaces SIMULTANEOUSLY plus K log lines — exactly the UI-dump FU4 promised to prevent. "One reveal per authored beat" is per-tick; "one reveal the player sees fade in" requires per-frame serialization, which the architecture has no mechanism for. The authored stagger is necessary but not sufficient.
confirmed.fix: Resolve the FU4 tension explicitly: either (a) have `tick` return an ORDERED list of reveal events and let the renderer drain at most one reveal-with-fade per animation frame (a renderer-side, NON-persisted display queue — distinct from the forbidden GameState `revealQueue`, so determinism is untouched), or (b) cap `tick`'s reveal-bearing advancement so at most one reveal-flagged tick is processed before a render. Document which, and add a `multiRevealFrames` check (below) so a batched-tick dump fails the headless harness instead of shipping silently.
confirmed.verdict: PARTIALLY REAL (core observation confirmed; severity overstated) — disposition trivial-fix. Citations verified: §2.1 FU4 (1157-1158) states the anti-dump guarantee at TICK granularity ("no two collide on a single tick") and forbids a runtime revealQueue; §6.9 (6132) render(state, prevState) is a single per-frame reconciliation; §6.3/§1.5 (5705-5708, 1217-1218) tick() takes whole-integer dtTicks that can be >1; §6.10 (6191-6200) tick(N)/frames(n)/toRung/toTier/advanceSeason/forceState compress many ticks. The genuine, reproducible gap: per-tick spacing does NOT formally imply per-render one-at-a-time, and the PRD nowhere specifies what the renderer does when ONE render(state, prevState) reconciliation surfaces K>1 newly-unlocked surfaces — that behavior is undefined, and the single-reconciliation render paints all diffs at once with no GameState queue. So FU4 proves the wrong invariant. HOWEVER the "breaks in live play" headline is overstated and 2 of 3 named vectors fail against the text: (1) backgrounded-then-foregrounded is REFUTED by line 6173 "Backgrounding pauses" + active-only/no-offline (no dtTicks burst on refocus); (2) "run for hours" is many small-dtTicks renders, not large per-render batches; (3) a janky frame would need to span the minutes-scale inter-reveal gap (reveal/deed cadence ~5 min, line 6587) to collide two reveals — a sub-second hitch cannot, a multi-minute one is a frozen tab; plus dense cold-open reveals are reduce-path (one intent -> one render, 5713), serialized by input not tick. The only real K>1 exposure is QA fast-forward (toTier/toRung/advanceSeason/forceState), which is INTENDED DEV state-compression for capture, not the player fade-in loop FU4 governs. Fix is trivial and FU4-compatible: FU4 bans only a PERSISTED revealQueue FIELD in GameState (5794-5798), not renderer-local transient animation sequencing (the renderer already animates results, 6134). A one-paragraph §2.1/§6.9 clarification suffices — state the real invariant (stagger margin >> max per-render batch in live play; background pauses prevent bursts), specify the renderer sequences fade-ins when one reconciliation surfaces K>1 new surfaces (presentation, not a GameState field), and note QA jumps render the destination directly and are exempt. No spine change, no human design call. Genuinely new vs rounds 1-2.

confirmed.title: Make the reveal engine headlessly testable: factor `render` into a pure `planReveals(state, prevState): RevealPlan` and assert it in Vitest (the differentiator currently has ZERO automated coverage)
confirmed.where: §6.9 (whole section, `src/ui/`, outside `npm run verify`), §6.10 (`reveals()`, `frames()`), §6.6.1 (verifier invariants), §6.12 (6252-6254 verify gate). Builds on the known 'renderer-outside-verify' open question with a concrete buildable hook.
confirmed.detail: Everything that makes the reveal engine THE differentiator — the `render(state, prevState)` reconciliation, one-at-a-time fade sequencing, once-per-game suppression, reveal-on-load idempotency, never-un-reveal monotonicity, the K/M/B formatter, responsive reflow — lives in `src/ui/` with "zero game logic" and is excluded from `npm run verify` (which tests pure core only). The sole renderer check is manual screenshot review via `capture-game-states` in a real browser — not CI. `__qa.reveals()` only proves the CORE flipped the right unlock STATE; it never proves the DOM revealed correctly, singly, or idempotently. The four defects above (doubly-specified truth, batched-tick dump, reveal-on-load, mid-animation) are all invisible to the current gate.
confirmed.fix: Split the renderer: a PURE `planReveals(state, prevState): RevealPlan { surfacesShownNow[], surfacesHiddenNow[], logLinesToFire[], animationsToStart[] }` (plain data, no DOM, no window) that lives core-adjacent and is unit-tested in Vitest under `verify`; the thin `applyPlan(plan, root)` is the only untested DOM step. Add assertions: (1) `surfacesHiddenNow` is ALWAYS empty (monotonicity — catches finding 1); (2) a `tick(N)` that flips K reveals yields a plan that serializes ≤1 reveal/frame or flags a multi-reveal frame (catches finding 2); (3) `planReveals(loaded, loaded)` is empty (catches finding 3); (4) re-applying the same plan is idempotent (catches finding 4); (5) `planReveals` is deterministic in the existing replay suite. Add `unrevealedSurfaceViolations` + `multiRevealFrames` to `VerifyReport`.
confirmed.verdict:
REAL, with one correction and a scope caveat; it is hard new evidence that SHARPENS the already-logged 'renderer-outside-verify' open question (ledger L119) rather than a brand-new blocker.

CONFIRMED from PRD text:
- The DOM reveal-TRANSITION behavior is outside `npm run verify`. §6.1 L5561 ("vitest unit-tests the pure core ... zero DOM"); §6.1 verify recipe L5590-5595 has no browser step; "no hosted CI" L6909/6944. The renderer is checked only by manual `capture-game-states` audits at milestone DoDs.
- `__qa.reveals()` (§6.10 L6199) is a CORE unlock-transition log ("reveal-cadence proxy"), and every DoD "revealing in order" assertion (L6550/6709/6753) reads it — never the DOM. So it proves the core flipped state, never that the DOM revealed singly/idempotently. TRUE.
- Two of the four named defects are genuinely uncovered by ANY gate: (a) BATCHED-TICK DUMP — §1 L88-90 states one-at-a-time is "by design (the unlock schedule is AUTHORED so reveals never arrive in a batch)", i.e. an authoring discipline, NOT a runtime serializer and NOT a §6.6.1 invariant (L5943-5969 lists none for "≤1 surface per atomic transition"); yet `__qa.tick(N)`/`frames()`/`toRung`/`toTier`/`advanceSeason` (L6192/6200) fast-forward large spans and a seasonal appraisal can cross multiple pillar gates at once. (b) REVEAL-ON-LOAD IDEMPOTENCY — `render(state, prevState)` with empty prevState on cold load would diff every visible surface as newly-revealed; nothing in spec asserts suppression. These two have no machine check anywhere.
- The proposed `planReveals(state, prevState): RevealPlan` factoring is genuinely absent and buildable: the PRD has the static pure `visibleSurfaces(state)` (§6.2 L5665) but no pure transition-PLANNER; pulling the reveal decision into a pure, vitest-asserted function fits D-013's pure-core boundary.

REFUTED / OVERSTATED parts (so the parent should not take the claim at face value):
- "Everything that makes the reveal engine THE differentiator lives in src/ui/ with zero game logic / has ZERO automated coverage" is too strong. The unlock TRUTH (`isUnlocked`/`visibleSurfaces`, §6.2) and the reveal LOG-LINE push (`core/rewards`+`reduce`, §6.3 L5713) already live in CORE and ARE vitest+verify covered; reveal ORDER is asserted off the core log. What truly lacks coverage is the DOM PRESENTATION of the transition (fade serialization, load-mount idempotency, mid-animation). "Responsive reflow" is the weakest leg — it has manual browser acceptance coverage (narrow-viewport same-order reveal, L6753; large-textScale reflow case, §6.11). And "mid-animation" is a DOM-runtime behavior a pure planReveals only partially closes.

DISPOSITION = needs-human (not trivial-fix): the fix is well-scoped but it (1) revises the §6.9 renderer contract (render consumes a RevealPlan), (2) adds a `core` reveal-planner module to §6.2, (3) needs a new §6.6.1-style invariant, and (4) turns on a real UX/architecture DESIGN call — what should happen when one atomic tick/load crosses N unlock thresholds (serialize over N frames? cap-per-frame? dump?) and what is the whitelist for legitimate multi-element reveals (the bar-by-bar four-bar Influence panel, the bespoke multi-reveals §2.1c L1176). ADR-worthy refinement, not a one-line edit.
