confirmed.title: Win-rate bands are a sampled probability with NO sampling protocol — the LOCKED 20–35% gate is either flaky CI or statistically under-powered
confirmed.where: §4.6.6 L4073-4077; §4.6.7 win-rate-band table L4101-4111; M2b combat unit test §7.2 L6628; M6 harness L6866; qa-playtesting.md §2-3. Combat RNG: §4.6.3 L4018 (±15% seeded), §4.6.4 L4030-4031 (separate crit/block rolls).
confirmed.detail: A 'win-rate' is P(win) over the combat RNG distribution — each fight draws many seeded rolls (hit/evade per swing, ±15% damage variance, crit, block) from the combat cursor, so ONE fixed-seed fight is a deterministic binary (win|lose), not a rate. To produce '20–35%' you must Monte-Carlo across a seed-set. Yet the spec (a) asserts the rate as a single number with no N, no fixed seed-set, no confidence policy ('a Vitest test confirms the win-rate sits in 20–35%', L6628), and (b) simultaneously demands 'a fixed-seed fight replays byte-identically' (L6628) — these need opposite setups (one seed vs many). Grep finds ZERO mentions of monte/sampling/seed-set/confidence/trials/N anywhere in prd.md, qa-playtesting.md, or fun-factor.md. Consequence: to assert the measured rate lies inside a 15-pt window the 95% CI half-width must be < ~0.075, needing N ≈ 1.96²·0.275·0.725/0.075² ≈ 136 fights per measured cell; at a naive N≈20 the CI half-width is 1.96·√(0.275·0.725/20) ≈ 0.196 → CI ≈ [0.08, 0.47], far wider than the band, so the gate passes/fails essentially at noise. The single instrument behind a LOCKED acceptance criterion is unspecified to the point of being non-reproducible.
confirmed.fix: Specify the win-rate measurement: a COMMITTED fixed seed-set (e.g., 200 seeds) so the gate is itself deterministic; assert the binomial point-estimate AND that its 95% CI is contained in the band (or assert the estimate ± a stated tolerance); state N per measured cell (R3/V2/V5/G1/G5 × fresh/trained × satiety). Separate the 'byte-identical replay' determinism test (1 seed) from the 'win-rate band' statistical test (seed-sweep) in §7.2 M2b and §4.6.7.
confirmed.verdict:
CONFIRMED — REAL and GENUINELY NEW. The round-3 claim survives adversarial refutation on every prong.

WHAT THE PRD TEXT PROVES (all line refs verified):
1. Win-rate IS a sampled probability over the combat cursor, not a deterministic scalar. §4.6.3 L4018 draws `rngVariance(0.85…1.15)` per swing from the combat cursor; §4.6.4 L4030-4031 draws SEPARATE seeded crit and block rolls per hit; §4.6.2 says a fight is ~6-12 swings. So each fight consumes a sequence of combat-cursor draws and resolves to a binary win|lose. A "20-35% win-rate" can ONLY be produced by sampling across many distinct seeds/cursor-starts (Monte Carlo). There is no closed-form P(win) in the spec, and §6.4 L5788 even notes mid-fight state is "NON-derivable" — confirming the process is genuinely stochastic, not analytically solvable. Refutation attempts (analytic rate? existence-not-rate test?) both fail: L6628/L6616 say "sits in 20-35%" — an explicit RATE assertion, and M6 L6869 gates on "a win-rate band outside its envelope."

2. The band is asserted with NO sampling protocol. §4.6.6 L4073-4090 and the §4.6.7 table L4101-4111 give a 15-point LOCKED band (R3 20-35%) but no N, no fixed seed-set, no tolerance/confidence policy. Grep over prd.md + qa-playtesting.md + fun-factor.md returns ZERO hits for monte/sampling/seed-set/confidence/trials/N-fights. The only near-miss — qa-playtesting §2 L100 "Run both per seed; run several seeds" — is (a) unquantified ("several") and (b) about the PACING auto-player optimal-vs-casual variants, NOT the combat win-rate proxy, which doesn't use the strategic bot at all (combat outcomes come from the combat cursor, not the action policy).

3. The M2a DoD conflates two opposite setups in one breath. L6628 (and L6614-6617) pair "a fixed-seed fight replays byte-identically" with "a Vitest test confirms the win-rate sits in 20-35%." The first needs ONE fixed seed; the second needs MANY. They aren't mutually exclusive as separate tests, but the spec never says the win-rate test is a multi-seed sample — leaving the single load-bearing instrument behind a LOCKED criterion under-specified to non-reproducibility.

4. It's wired as a HARD BUILD GATE, not report-only. M6 phase-4 L6869 fails the build on "a win-rate band outside its R3/V2/V5/G1/G5 envelope" — so a noise-driven instrument can flip CI.

STATISTICS VERIFIED (reproduced exactly): for p=0.275, CI half-width <0.075 needs N≈136 fights per measured cell; at a naive N≈20 the 95% half-width is 0.196 → CI ≈ [0.08, 0.47], far wider than the 15-pt band; even N=100 gives ±0.0875, still exceeding the band. So at any plausible test N the gate passes/fails essentially at noise — flaky CI or statistically under-powered, exactly as claimed.

NOT a duplicate of rounds 1-2. Distinct from the logged "fun-proxy-blank-thresholds" (ledger L113 — that is about literally BLANK values "~N s"/"~X min" for dead-time/reward-cadence; the win-rate band value is NOT blank, it's 20-35% — the defect here is a missing MEASUREMENT METHODOLOGY for a stochastic quantity, not a missing number). Distinct from "bot-policy-one-sentence" (ledger L114 — that is the auto-player's action-selection heuristic; the combat win-rate is pure combat-RNG sampling that doesn't route through the strategic bot). The novel defect: a sampled-probability acceptance gate with no N / seed-set / tolerance, plus the one-seed-determinism vs many-seed-rate conflation.

DISPOSITION — needs-human (small call, big leverage). The fix is a short spec clause, BUT it requires a deliberate QA-methodology decision that defines how a HUMAN-LOCKED criterion is actually enforced: pick N (≈400-500 fixed canonical seeds for comfortable margin), define the seed-set, and choose the pass rule — point-estimate-in-band (loose) vs CI-fully-in-band (needs very large N, borderline unachievable for a 15-pt window) vs band-with-tolerance. Because strict CI-containment of a 15-point band is statistically demanding and the band is human-LOCKED, whether the gate is strict-but-flaky or loose-but-stable is a "how strictly do we enforce your locked 20-35%" question that should be confirmed with the human rather than silently defaulted. Recommended default to put in front of them: "win-rate = empirical win-fraction over a FIXED canonical seed-set of N≥500 seeds; the gate asserts the point estimate ∈ [0.20,0.35]; the M2a byte-identical-replay test is explicitly a SEPARATE single-seed determinism test." Land that in §4.6.6/§4.6.7 + the M2a/M6 DoD and the gate becomes reproducible.

confirmed.title: The ≥30-min floor is tick-count × the SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN lever — the bot measures the tautological image of a lever, not real dwell or engagement
confirmed.where: Wall-clock binding §4.0/L3217-3223 + L3260; SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN lever §4.9 L4474, L4514; floor 'measured' as 'per-rung tick-count floor' §4.8.2 L4322 and 'tick-count to the wolf' L4323; M6 harness 'per-rung tick→wall-clock report' L6866; active-only loop L6548; FU23 'leave it running' L948.
confirmed.detail: The floor target is real minutes, but the bot plays 'in seconds' and never waits real-time (§2 qa-playtesting). It measures abstract TICK-COUNT and reconstructs 'wall-clock minutes' by multiplying by SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN — itself an explicit lever (L3222, L4474). So the undershoot gate reduces to comparing two configured numbers: (rung-meter clear-condition in ticks) ≥ (30 min worth of ticks = 30 ÷ min-per-tick). Both sides are dials; the gate can only catch an arithmetic mismatch between them (a coding bug), never an emergent pacing problem. Worse for VALIDITY: under the active-only model the clock advances with real elapsed time while the tab is focused and auto-resolve/auto-repeat runs (FU23), so a real 30-min rung can elapse with the player IDLE — the floor measures elapsed real-time (mechanically pinned by the clock rate), not that the player had meaningful actions. The bot, ticking instantly and modelling no human attention, cannot distinguish '30 min of engaged play' from '30 min of idle auto-advance.' Nothing in the harness measures action-density-per-rung — the quantity the 30-min floor actually exists to protect. (Distinct from B4, which is the per-rung scalar vs escalating-clock mismatch; this is the instrument-validity gap in HOW wall-clock is derived at all.)
confirmed.fix: State explicitly that the pacing 'wall-clock' is a derived display (ticks × SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN), not an emergent measurement, and that the undershoot gate is therefore an internal-consistency check, not a human-dwell check. Add a separate engagement proxy that the bot CAN compute: required-player-intents-per-rung and the longest stretch the bot is forced to take a no-op tick (the only bot-observable analogue of dead-time) — and gate on that, not on multiplied wall-clock alone.
confirmed.verdict:
REAL (core confirmed; two prongs overstated — record the narrower, sharper finding). NEW, distinct from B4 and from the logged open questions.

CONFIRMED FROM TEXT — the instrument-validity gap is genuine:
- The rung floor is tautological by construction: §4.1.1 L3396 `thresholdForRung = RUNG_FLOOR_MIN(30) · eligibleActivityRate`; L3401-02 calls the meter and the time-floor "the same constraint expressed two ways." Fill-time = (30·rate)/rate = 30 "min" at the intended rate.
- Every "minutes" figure is tick-count × a FREE lever: §4.0 L3217-23 binds wall-clock⇄game-time and states the binding "itself is a lever (SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN[tier])"; §4.9 L4474/L4514 = 34/60/120. The gate is the "per-rung tick-count floor" (L4322); the M6 report is "per-rung tick→wall-clock" (L6866).
- The bot is real-time-blind by construction: qa-playtesting L81/L93/L98 fast-forwards "to T2 in seconds, not hours" and injects tick(dtTicks) directly (PRD L6548). The real app derives dtTicks from real elapsed active time (L5736/L6170-71); the harness never does. So the tick↔real-minute binding ("1 season ≈ 34 real min") is an ASSERTED designer constant, never measured — and structurally cannot be, by a bot that ticks instantly.
- Active-only + FU23 (L945-50, L6170-71): auto-resolve/auto-repeat tick the loop while the tab is merely open, so even a real human's "30 min" is satisfiable by idle auto-advance. The floor protects elapsed-clock dwell, not action.

Net consequence (the NEW finding, sharper than the claim as written): a SINGLE mis-set SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN / tick-mapping lever silently rescales the ENTIRE minutes-denominated acceptance battery (≥30-min/rung floor, 4.5/8/16h tier budgets, AND the fun-proxy dead-time/deed-cadence thresholds) and every undershoot gate still passes — a single point of failure the harness cannot detect. qa L101 frames the budget as "hard data, not vibes," which over-claims what a real-time-blind bot can validate; it proves a tick-count, then relabels it hours via a dial.

TWO OVERSTATEMENTS to record narrower (don't carry them as fact):
1. "Gate can ONLY catch a coding bug." No — the harness runs REAL intents through the REAL engine (L6548; qa L95-97), so the per-rung tick-count is EMERGENT from the curated-activity action definitions (point-yields, tick-costs, PER_EVENT_CAP_FRACTION). It genuinely catches content-throughput drift from the authored rate (qa L98-99 "isn't trivially broken") — a real, if narrow, balance-bug class.
2. "NOTHING measures action-density." The SEPARATE fun-proxy gate measures dead-time / reward-unlock cadence / deed-cadence (qa L117-125) — those ARE the engagement instruments; the 30-min floor was never meant to be one. (Caveat that REINFORCES the finding: those fun-proxy thresholds are also tick×lever and currently blank.)

FIX SPLIT — needs-human:
- Trivial doc caveat (does NOT close the gap, only stops over-claiming): one line in §4.8/qa-playtesting stating the M6 pacing + fun gates validate TICK-SPACE dwell against the asserted binding, NOT real wall-clock; binding calibration is a human-playtest responsibility, not CI-checkable; drop or qualify the "hard data, not vibes" framing.
- The real call (human design decision): the validity question "does ~6000 ticks actually equal ~34 real human minutes?" is unanswerable by a headless instant-tick bot BY CONSTRUCTION. Closing it requires choosing either (i) accept the binding as a designer constant and add a PERIODIC HUMAN-SESSION CALIBRATION checkpoint that measures real ticks/min on a live session and feeds SEASON_WALLCLOCK_MIN back (the only way to anchor the whole battery to reality), or (ii) re-express the floor/budgets natively in TICKS and stop the gate claiming it validates real-time dwell. Scope-of-claim design call, not a code typo.

confirmed.title: No itemized deed supply for the great/excellent bands — EVERY v1 tier-gate is unclearable as costed, yet the accrual verifier passes
confirmed.where: §4.1 bands table L3336-3360 + tie-out note L3352-3360; §4.2.1 deed inventory L3498-3531 ("sums EXACTLY to 70% of GOOD"); §4.2.2 seasonal cap L3591-3623; §6.6.1 verifier L5944-5949
confirmed.detail: The §4.2.1 deed inventory is sized to sum to EXACTLY 70% of each pillar's GOOD band, and the §4.2.2 seasonal stream is hard-capped at 30% of GOOD (f_pillar∈[0,1], telescopes to JUDGE_K·1 = 0.30·good — it can NEVER exceed 30% of good). So the only itemized content = deeds(70%)+seasonal(30%) = exactly 100% of the GOOD band, no more. But EVERY tier-gate requires supra-good bands: T0 (2-pillar special) needs 'good in both + EXCELLENT in 1' — cheapest clear = good Estate (0.8K) + excellent Arms (0.95K), and Arms' entire itemized supply is 350 deeds + 150 seasonal = 500 ip = the good band, so excellent Arms needs +450 ip that nothing supplies. T1 needs 'great in 2' (Estate great 11K vs itemized 8K → +3K; Arms great 7.5K vs 5K → +2.5K). T2 needs Estate great/excellent (84K/112K vs itemized 60K → +24K/+52K) and Office great (78K vs 50K → +28K). The §6.6.1 'accrual tie-out' check (L5947-5949) verifies deeds sum to 70%-of-GOOD, and 'gate-monotonicity' (L5944) asserts 'the gate band is achievable from available deeds' — but the only countable deeds reach GOOD, so the verifier either passes vacuously or is checking the wrong band while every gate is structurally unclearable. The Q23 'more deeds welcome / no fixed budget' hand-wave is the intended supply, but it is uncounted, untied-out, and the deed schema (§2.12 L1769) has no repeatable/one-shot/count-cap field — so the supply that would make excellent reachable is exactly the supply that, if it exists, breaks the 70/30 tie-out the verifier asserts. The two requirements are mutually exclusive under one itemized inventory.
confirmed.fix: Either (a) author and itemize a SEPARATE bounded great/excellent deed inventory per pillar/tier (a specialisation surplus that ties out to the excellent band) and have the gate-monotonicity verifier check excellent-reachability against it; or (b) define per-deed-type repeatability + a hard count-cap on repeatable deed-jumps so the supply is bounded and the 70/30 split + Phase-2 floor stay binding. Add a `repeatable`/`maxAwards` field to `Quest`/`AccrualEvent` and a deed-completion ledger; make §6.6.1 assert reachability of the actual required band (great/excellent), not just GOOD.
confirmed.verdict:
CONFIRMED (with one framing correction) — REAL, reproducible, and NEW. Tried to refute; couldn't. The claim's kernel holds against the text; only its headline word "unclearable" needs gloss.

THE REPRODUCIBLE CORE (all line-cited in /Users/raynos/projects/games/kami-kakushi/docs/prd.md):
1. The ONLY supply the PRD costs/itemizes/back-solves tops out at the GOOD band:
   - Deeds = EXACTLY 70% of good, itemized line-by-line (§4.2.1 L3504-3527: T0 Estate 560/Arms 350; T1 5,600/3,500/1,400; T2 42,000/21,000/35,000).
   - Seasonal = EXACTLY 30% of good and HARD-CAPPED there: seasonalScore = JUDGE_K·f, f=sqrt(clamp(basis/TIER_REF,0,1))∈[0,1], so the high-water gain telescopes to JUDGE_K·1 = 0.30·good (§4.2.2 L3591-3623). Seasonal can NEVER contribute one ip above good — the entire great/excellent surplus must therefore come from deeds.
   - Deeds(70%) + seasonal(30%) = 100% of GOOD, no more.
2. EVERY v1 gate requires supra-good (§4.1 L3336-3346): T0 = good in both + excellent in 1; T1 = great in 2; T2 = Estate/Office great/excellent. Cheapest clears all need ip the costed supply does not contain — e.g. T0 excellent Arms 0.95K vs costed 0.5K (+450 ip, all of which must be deeds since seasonal is pinned at 150); T1 Estate great 11K vs 8K (+3K); T2 Estate excellent 112K vs 60K (+52K), Office great 78K vs 50K (+28K). Arithmetic verified.
3. The gate-clearing surplus is uncosted on EVERY axis:
   - ip-uncounted: §4.2.1 only ever itemizes the 70%-of-good count and calls it "the minimum that ties out" (L3529); the surplus is named solely as a LEVER ("the great/excellent specialisation deed-counts," L3535) and never given a value.
   - TIME-unbudgeted: §4.8.1b's "⏱ Phase-2 window" (L4383-4387) = the good-band deed cadence EXACTLY — 56/90/72 deeds × ~5/8/13 min = 4.67h/12h/15.6h = the stated ~4.5-5h/~12h/~15.5h. The "great/excellent reached by additional capped deeds" column carries NO count and NO minutes; the window is 100% consumed buying GOOD, which clears no gate.
   - schema-unrepresentable: §2.12 `Quest { id, type, suggestionText, openEnded, advanceEvents[], rewards: RewardBundle, gatesSpine }` (L1769) has no repeatable / instance-count / count-cap / diminishing field; one `RewardBundle` reads ONE-SHOT, so the itemized inventory reads as the total finite supply.
   - verifier-untieable: §6.6.1 (L5944-5949) asserts BOTH "the gate band is achievable from the tier's available deeds" (gate-monotonicity) AND "the deed inventory sums to the gate share within tolerance" (accrual tie-out) — but §4.2.1/§4.8.1b fix that inventory at the GOOD share. The two assertions cannot both honestly pass against the itemized data: the deed/seasonal tie-out proves GOOD, the §4.1 bands demand GREAT/EXCELLENT, and §4.8.1b L4380-4381 even admits "only the §4.2.1/§4.2.2 deed/seasonal tie-outs and the §4.1 hybrid bands are load-bearing" — i.e. it asserts the 70/30-of-good tie-out AND the supra-good gate while supplying only the former. The claim's "two requirements mutually exclusive under one itemized inventory" is literally what the verifier asserts.

FRAMING CORRECTION (why "unclearable" is half-right): at RUNTIME the gate is NOT a softlock — auto-repeat (FU23, L948) lets a player grind more capped nest-clears, and Q23 ("more deeds welcome / no fixed budget," L3529-3531) is the intended surplus source. So it's not literally unclearable. But the claim already says "unclearable AS COSTED," and that gloss is exactly right: everything the PRD costs, itemizes, time-budgets, schematizes and machine-verifies stops at GOOD; the supply that actually clears every gate is entirely uncosted, untimed, unschematized, and un-tie-out-able. The defect is a specification/verification hole, not a player-facing softlock.

NEW vs rounds 1-2: distinct from B1 (Name auto-CLEARS its gate with zero deeds — the inverse problem, and Name-specific); from Q2 (total budget ~60h vs 28.5h is a 2×-undercount of a window that at least exists — here the gate-clearing grind is OUTSIDE the costed window entirely); and from Q5 (breadth wall blocks a specialiser on a LAGGING pillar — here it's the great/excellent surplus on the CHOSEN pillar that has no costed supply). Not in B1-B7 or any logged open question. Grep of brainstorms/2026-06-26-prd-v2-audit.md confirms it is unlogged.

WHY needs-human (not trivial-fix): resolution requires design+balance+schema decisions, parallel to B1/B4/B5 — (a) quantify the great/excellent surplus deed-counts per pillar/tier (the unset L3535 lever); (b) add the minutes they add to the Phase-2 window (compounding Q2's budget headline with a NEW, distinct mechanism — the gate-clearing grind currently has zero budget); (c) add a deed-schema repeatability/diminishing field to §2.12 so "more capped deeds" is representable and bounded; (d) redefine §6.6.1's two checks so reachability of a supra-good gate is a Phase-2-window TIME budget (auto-repeat-rate × window ≥ surplus), not an inventory-sum, and reword accrual tie-out so "gate share" doesn't read as "good share." A one-line edit can't settle the model. → suggest a new blocking item (B8) and a human question parallel to Q6/Q7.

confirmed.title: A scripted Estate dent transiently breaks the trade-≤⅓ HARD invariant (sub-engine distribution unspecified; verifier proves post-combo but not post-dent)
confirmed.where: §4.2.3 working form L3673-3683; §4.2.4 dent L3697-3712; §2.16 `Dent{pillar,amount}` + `subEngines{land,treasury,trade}` L2071-2076; §2.19 persists BOTH pillar value AND subEngines L2233; §6.6.1 trade proof L5957-5959
confirmed.detail: The HARD invariant is implemented as `tradeAllowed = 0.5·(land+treasury); trade = min(rawTrade, tradeAllowed)`, re-clamped only AT accrual points. But §4.2.4 dents remove `DENT_FRACTION=0.10` of a PILLAR's value (the canonical Estate dent = 'a called debt'), and `Dent{pillar,amount}` (L2076) targets the pillar with a scalar — it does NOT say which of land/treasury/trade absorbs it. If a called-debt dent reduces treasury (the diegetically correct strand) without touching trade, the denominator (land+treasury) shrinks while trade is unchanged, so trade momentarily exceeds ⅓ of the new estate total. Worked: land=100, treasury=100, trade=100 (=⅓ of 300). Treasury dents −10 → estateTotal=290, trade/total = 100/290 = 34.5% > ⅓, and it stays violated through the ~1-2 seasons the dent self-heals (no re-clamp fires until the next trade accrual). The §6.6 verifier proves trade ≤⅓ 'EVEN AFTER cross-pillar combos' (L5957-5959) but is silent on the dent path. Alternatively, if the dent reduces the Estate pillar `value` scalar but not the sub-engines (both are persisted separately, L2233), `value` desyncs from `land+treasury+trade`, corrupting the clamp denominator and the §6.6.1 proof on next load.
confirmed.fix: Specify dent distribution: either (a) distribute an Estate dent proportionally across land/treasury/trade (preserves the ratio exactly), or (b) if it hits a single strand, re-run the trade clamp immediately after any dent. Make the §6.6 verifier assert trade ≤⅓ post-DENT as well as post-combo. Pin the invariant `estate.value == land+treasury+trade` as a verifier check so the scalar and sub-engines can't desync.
confirmed.verdict:
REAL (with a mechanism correction), needs-human. The dent×trade-clamp interaction is genuinely unspecified and breaks the HARD trade-≤⅓ invariant on the dent path — but via the claim's SECOND horn, not its first.

DATA MODEL (decisive): canonical GameState type at prd.md L5775-5777 — influence is Record<PillarId,{value,highWater,dent:Dent|null, subEngines?:{land,treasury,trade}}>, SubEngine={value,highWater}. The dent is ONE Dent|null attached at the PILLAR level; §4.2.4 L3703 removes DENT_FRACTION=0.10 of the pillar `value`. Sub-engines are stored separately.

HORN A (claim's primary: dent reduces the treasury sub-engine, shrinking land+treasury so trade momentarily exceeds ⅓) — REFUTED as written. Dent targets the pillar, not a sub-engine; the working-form denominator 0.5·(land+treasury) (L3675) reads sub-engine values a pillar-value dent never touches. Horn A only occurs if an implementer re-routes a called-debt dent into the treasury strand (diegetically tempting, not the spec).

HORN B (dent reduces pillar `value`, sub-engines untouched → desync) — CONFIRMED, this is the spec's model. estateWealth.value -= 0.10·value while land/treasury/trade stay full. Then (1) value desyncs from land+treasury+trade (the pillar total IS the strand sum), and (2) the §6.6 invariant, stated as 'trade sub-engine ≤⅓ of THAT PILLAR' (L5907) i.e. against the pillar value, transiently breaks: land=treasury=trade=100, value dents 300→270, trade.value stays 100 → 100/270≈37%>⅓, holding ~1-2 seasons until the seasonal-restore branch (L3704-3706) regrows value. A trade re-accrual does NOT reconcile it — the clamp re-evaluates trade against 0.5·(land+treasury) (sub-engines), not the dented value.

CLAIM WORDING CORRECTION: Horn B does NOT 'corrupt the clamp denominator' — the working-form denominator (land+treasury) is sub-engine-based and immune to a value-only dent. What breaks is the value-vs-strand-sum identity plus every Estate-total reader that uses the pillar value (the §6.6 proof, the gate/panel total).

COVERAGE GAP: §6.6.1 verifier proves trade≤⅓ 'even after cross-pillar combos' (L5907-5909, L5957-5959) but has no dent case, and is a BUILD-TIME content check (verify-content.ts L5900) that never sees runtime dented states. M0 task L6704 tests 'trade≤⅓ holds' and 'dent self-heals' as SEPARATE assertions — nothing tests trade≤⅓ across a dent. The §4.2.3 proof's 'trade/estateTotal<1/3 always' (L3683) silently assumes land+treasury/value never decrease, which a dent violates.

SECONDARY (new, adjacent): M0 task L6704 writes the clamp as 'tradeAllowed=⅓·estateTotal' — the CIRCULAR form §4.2.3 L3674 explicitly replaced with 0.5·(land+treasury). They coincide only at the binding fixpoint; on a below-cap state (land=treasury=100,trade=0) they diverge (100 vs 66.7). A verbatim implementer mis-builds the cap. One-line reconciliation.

NOT trivially fixable: resolution forces a design choice on how a pillar-level dent maps onto the Estate strand representation while preserving a CANON, 'NOT tunable' invariant (L3688) and a human-locked diegetic dent-cause (called debt→Estate). ≥3 defensible options: (a) scale all sub-engines ×0.9 proportionally (preserves ratio+value=sum, but a 'called debt' implausibly shrinks land and trade); (b) hit a named strand + add the dent to the clamp's re-fire set (diegetic+safe, but reintroduces Horn A absent a mandated immediate re-clamp); (c) make Estate value purely derived =land+treasury+trade, never stored (cleanest, but contradicts L2233/L5775 which persist BOTH value AND sub-engines). It also forces resolving the storage-authority ambiguity (is Estate value authoritative or derived?) a subagent should not settle. Implementation once decided is small.

Locations: §4.2.3 L3667-3695; §4.2.4 L3697-3712; data shape L2071-2077, L2233; type L5775-5777; §6.6 L5907-5909; §6.6.1 L5957-5959; M0 task L6704.
